perturbation.original_text:
3.2.13 In the cases that Party B guarantees to sign the Financial Leasing Agreement with Party A's users, Party B will agree on the provisions of terminating the Financial Leasing Agreement unilaterally by the Driver User in advance with the Driver User according to the conditions or better conditions specified in Annex 2 Terms and Conditions for the Driver User to Terminate Financial Leasing Agreement in Advance, and the relevant provisions shall at least comply with the following agreements:
3.2.13.1 Party B shall clarify the logic and method for calculating the liquidated damages due to the unilateral termination of the Financial Leasing Agreement by the Driver User;
3.2.13.2 After signing the agreement with Party B, the Driver User is entitled to terminate the Financial Leasing Agreement 7 days in advance in the last week (7 natural days) of every three months.
3.2.13.3 In case that the Driver User unilaterally terminates the Financial Leasing Agreement in advance, he/she does not need to pay the remaining rent and the last payment;
3.2.13.4 In case that the Driver User unilaterally terminates the Financial Leasing Agreement in advance, Party B shall collect the liquidated damages from the Driver User within 30% of the "down payment + total monthly rent + last payment" stipulated in the Financial Leasing Agreement; If major maintenance (maintenance fee is RMB3,000 or above) of the leased vehicle occurs during the lease period, Party B may charge the depreciation expense of the Driver User's rental of the vehicle in addition to the liquidated damages mentioned above, and the depreciation expense for each time shall be less than 20% of the current maintenance fee. (Depreciation expense can only be charged in that the Driver User unilaterally terminates the Agreement in advance without paying the full amount to buy the vehicle.)
3.2.13.5 Party B shall refund all the monthly rental paid by the Driver User in accordance with the Financial Leasing Agreement and all the other expenses excluding that paid to the third party (e.g. vehicle purchase tax, insurance premium, etc.).
3.2.13.6 Party B shall explain the conditions for the termination of the Agreement to the Driver User, fully communicate to reach consensus, and obtain the signature confirmation from the Driver User.
3.2.13.7 In case that Party B has different conditions for termination of agreement for different types of vehicles, they shall all conform to the provisions of this agreement, and the written consent of Party A shall be obtained in advance before they can be applied to the Financial Leasing Agreement signed with the Driver User.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2.13 In the cases that Party B guarantees to sign the Financial Leasing Agreement with Party A's users, Party B will make reasonable efforts to allow the Driver User to terminate the Financial Leasing Agreement. The specifics of termination will be decided at Party B's discretion.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes the specific consumer protections regarding early termination of financial leasing agreements, granting Party B full discretion and removing the user's right to terminate the agreement under specific conditions, thus contradicting consumer protection laws.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Consumer Rights Protection Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_citation: Article 24 of the Consumer Rights Protection Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: By removing the consumer's right to terminate the agreement under pre-defined favorable conditions (limiting liquidated damages, refunding rentals, etc.), the modified text violates Article 24 of the Consumer Rights Protection Law of China, which stipulates that business operators shall not, by means of standard contracts, make unjust or unreasonable provisions that are disadvantageous to consumers.

perturbation.justification: The original text outlined specific consumer protections regarding early termination of financial leasing agreements. The changed text eliminates these protections, granting Party B full discretion. The Law Explanation states that this removal violates Article 24 of the Consumer Rights Protection Law of China, which prohibits unjust or unreasonable provisions disadvantageous to consumers. While the scraped snippets are unhelpful, the 'Perturbation Explanation' and 'Law Explanation', alongside the original and modified text comparison, clearly indicate a contradiction by removing consumer rights. Therefore, the changes contradict consumer protection laws by removing the legally mandated protection.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2.4 Party B shall be obliged to verify the actual use of the leased vehicles, assist and ensure that the Driver User purchases corresponding insurance according to the actual use of the leased vehicles; if the leased vehicle is used for online Ride- hailing operation, in case that the provisions of the local Laws on online Ride-hailing insurance are changed within the term of cooperation or the provisions of the local Laws on online Ride-hailing are officially implemented within the term of cooperation, Party B shall inform the Driver User within 3 working days after the change of local Laws and regulations on Ride-hailing or the effective implementation thereof, assist the Driver User and ensure that the insurance of the leased vehicle shall be changed in accordance with the provisions of local Laws on ride-hailing to make the insurance of the leased vehicle comply with the provisions of relevant Laws and regulations on Ride-hailing at that time.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2.4 Party B shall be obliged to verify the actual use of the leased vehicles; if the leased vehicle is used for online Ride- hailing operation, Party B may inform the Driver User of the potential need for changes in insurance coverage.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the obligation for Party B to 'assist and ensure' that the Driver User purchases the correct insurance and stays compliant with local ride-hailing laws, this change creates a legal risk and contradicts insurance requirements for commercial vehicles and ride-hailing services.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Regulations on the Administration of Online Taxi Booking Services (China)
perturbation.law_citation: Article 17 of the Regulations on the Administration of Online Taxi Booking Services
perturbation.law_explanation: Article 17 states that online ride-hailing platforms must ensure their vehicles have adequate insurance, making them responsible for the provision of insurance to the driver. The modification removes this obligation and makes it non-compliant, putting the burden of insurance on the driver and creating an operational safety hazard.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: statute | Wex | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you statute A statute is a law enacted by a legislature. Statutes are also called acts, such as the Civil Rights Act of 1964 or the Sarbanes-Oxley Act . Federal laws must be passed by both houses of Congress, the House of Representative and the Senate, and then usually require approval from the President before they can take effect. As explained by the Library of Congress , enacted federal statutes are published multiple times. First, each individual law is published as a “slip law.” Then, all of the slip laws for each session of Congress are published together as “session laws.” Finally, all laws that are of a “general and permanent nature” are eventually compiled into the United States Code , and also the Revised Statutes of the United States. State statutes can be found through the LII's page on Constitutions, Statutes, and Codes . Readers can also attempt to find state statutes organized by topic: Topical Index: State Statutes . [Last reviewed in May of 2020 by the Wex Definitions Team ] Wex CIVICS wex definitions government legal education and practice statutes Wex Toolbox
perturbation.justification: The modified text removes Party B's obligation to 'assist and ensure' the Driver User purchases compliant insurance, weakening the original obligation and creating a legal risk. The Law Explanation states that ride-hailing platforms must ensure adequate insurance, making them responsible for the provision of insurance to the driver. The omission of Party B's responsibility directly contradicts this principle, shifting the burden of insurance compliance entirely to the driver, which may not fulfill the legal requirements for commercial ride-hailing services.

perturbation.original_text: 3.2.15 In case that the vehicle leased by Party B to the Driver User is used for the online Ride-Hailing Service, where a traffic accident or other safety accident occurs when a leased vehicle provides an online Ride-Hailing Service, if passengers or other subjects in traffic accidents or safety accidents (subjects other than the Driver User who rent vehicles and passengers) require the Driver User to Compensate For Their Personal Injury, or if the Driver User fails to pay or is unable to pay while the carriage Agreement losses, or if the passenger requests the Driver User or other subjects to bear the carriage liability of the online Ride-hailing Service, Party B shall deal with these situations timely, assume the carrier responsibility of leasing the vehicle which provides online Ride-Hailing Service and pay the compensation in time; If it is the Driver User's responsibility, Party B may recover it from the Driver User.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.2.15 In case that the vehicle leased by Party B to the Driver User is used for the online Ride-Hailing Service, where a traffic accident or other safety accident occurs when a leased vehicle provides an online Ride-Hailing Service, if passengers or other subjects in traffic accidents or safety accidents (subjects other than the Driver User who rent vehicles and passengers) require the Driver User to Compensate For Their Personal Injury, Party B will advise the Driver User on legal recourse.
perturbation.explanation: This change removes Party B's obligation to assume carrier responsibility and provide compensation in case of accidents, shifting the burden entirely to the driver and potentially leaving injured parties without recourse. This is a violation of carrier liability principles.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_citation: Article 302 of the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
perturbation.law_explanation: By removing Party B's responsibility as the carrier to compensate for damages or injuries during online ride-hailing services, the modified text violates Article 302 of the Contract Law. This article holds carriers responsible for injuries or property loss sustained by passengers during transportation, unless the damage is proven to be caused by the passenger's own health or intentional misconduct.

Contract Law of the People's Republic of China was a law in the PRC that came into force on October 1, 1999, and was abolished on Jan 1, 2021 with the enforcement of Civil Code of the People's Republic of China . It is the main source of contract law in the PRC. The most recent version combines many parts of Chinese contract law that were previously parts of other substantive laws, and has led to increased legal uniformity. The law includes both general and specific provisions and also has the legal rules that relate to the formation, fulfilment and breach of contracts. The law allows for various different types of contracts that can be entered into. [ 1 ] "The principles of the freedom of contract, of good faith, and of the fostering of transactions have informed and guided the formulation of the Contract Law and are embodied in many of its major provisions." [ 2 ] The main purpose of the law is stated in Article 1: "This Law is enacted for the purpose of protecting the legitimate rights and interests of the parties to contracts, maintaining the socio-economic order and promoting the socialist modernization." [ 3 ] Article 2 has the definition for a contract: "For the purpose of this Law, a contract means an agreement on the establis
perturbation.justification: The modified contract removes Party B's responsibility to compensate for damages or injuries during online ride-hailing services, shifting the responsibility to advise the driver user on legal recourse. The Omission Legal Contradiction definition states that a contradiction occurs when a contract omits a legally mandated consumer protection, making it non-compliant. The law explanation states that this contradicts Article 302 of the Contract Law, holding carriers responsible for injuries or property loss. Snippet 2 supports this, stating the Contract Law of the People's Republic of China was the main source of contract law in the PRC, outlining rules for breach of contracts. The original text clearly outlines Party B's carrier responsibility, which is completely omitted from the changed text, creating a clear contradiction of carrier responsibility.
